title: Define partially legible mapping rules

tests.description: mapSignalsToVisuals returns a complete VisualParams object with all expected keys
All output values are plain numbers — no strings or raw identifiers leak through
Geo mapping — different countries produce different palette hues
Geo mapping — same country and seed produces consistent hue
Geo mapping — paletteHue is in 0-360 range, paletteSaturation in 0-1 range
Time mapping — night hours produce slower cadence than afternoon hours
Time mapping — cadence value is always positive
Density mapping — higher DPR and more cores produce higher density
Density mapping — output is clamped to safe range
Reduced-motion preference — returns low amplitude when true
Reduced-motion preference — null defaults to full amplitude
Pointer disturbance — returns 0 when pointer is inactive
Pointer disturbance — scales with pointer speed when active
Bass mapping — returns 0 for silent input
Treble mapping — returns 0 for silent input
Bass and treble mappings — scale correctly from 0-255 to 0-1
Null and missing inputs produce safe fallback values — no throws
No forbidden storage APIs accessed during mapping
Output contains no raw identifiers — country codes, timezone strings, or user-agent fragments
mapSignalsToVisuals is a pure function — same inputs always yield same outputs
At least 4 distinct signal-to-visual mappings are exercised

imports: import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest';
describeBlock: US-008: Define partially legible mapping rules
